text: Intel Crab: UNIFIL peacekeeper killed near Adchit Al-Qusayr is strategically significant — Indonesia has the largest UNIFIL contingent (~1,200 troops) and Jakarta has been a vocal opponent of Israel's campaign in Lebanon. UNIFIL statement language ('may amount to war crimes') unusually strong. Watch for Indonesian political response and potential UNIFIL restructuring.
verificationNote: UNIFIL statement confirmed by RTE, Times of Israel, ANI News. Indonesia battalion confirmed by UNIFIL.

text: Iran War Live: Day 30 evening summary (7:12 PM PDT) — (1) IDF evening Tehran wave: 120+ bombs, power blackouts across 4 districts, 2 killed near Shaft; (2) IAEA confirms Khondab IR-40 destroyed; (3) UNIFIL peacekeeper killed, Adchit Al-Qusayr; (4) Houthi 2nd wave: 2 drones intercepted; (5) Trump: Iran agreed to 'most of' 15 points, Kharg Island still on table; (6) Islamabad summit: Pakistan ready to host Rubio-Araghchi talks; (7) Iran advancing NPT withdrawal legislation; cumulative: Iran 1,972+ killed, Lebanon 1,239+ killed.
verificationNote: Compiled from Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, CNN, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, NPR Day 30 liveblogs.

text: Iran War Live: Day 31 opening (5:27 AM PDT) — (1) IDF dropped 120+ bombs overnight on Tehran missile R&D sites, power blackouts in 4 districts, 2 killed near Shaft; (2) Tabriz Petrochemical Company struck at midnight Tehran time, fire contained, CEO confirms live on state TV; (3) Mehrabad Airport re-struck; (4) Iranian/Hezbollah barrage ignites fire at Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery (7th Iranian wave of night); (5) Indian worker killed in Iranian drone strike on Kuwait power/desalination plant; (6) IDF Alpinist Unit conducted first documented cross-border raid from Syrian Hermon into south Lebanon.
verificationNote: Compiled from Times of Israel, Anadolu Agency, Al Jazeera, Jerusalem Post, Kuwait Times Day 31 coverage.

text: Diplomacy Watch: Trump's Day 31 FT interview contains the most internally contradictory diplomatic messaging of the conflict: (A) claims 'regime change achieved' and Iran agreed to 'most of' 15 US demands; (B) simultaneously threatens to seize Kharg Island and 'obliterate' Iran's oil infrastructure. Iran publicly denied accepting any US demands. Analysis: classic Trump simultaneous pressure/olive-branch messaging. The actual forcing function is April 6 Hormuz deadline. Rubio-Araghchi meeting in Islamabad reportedly still on track for 'coming days.'
verificationNote: Trump FT interview confirmed by Bloomberg, NBC News, Time Magazine. Iran denial confirmed by ANI News citing Araghchi statements. Pakistan FM Dar's statement on Rubio-Araghchi talks per Al Jazeera.

text: NATO Affairs Monitor: Spain's formal airspace closure to US military planes in the Iran war is the most significant intra-NATO rupture since the conflict began. Three developments in sequence: (1) Spain barred use of Rota and Morón bases; (2) US relocated 15 aircraft; (3) Spain now closed all Spanish airspace to US planes in the campaign. Trump threatening trade war with Spain escalates this into an economic confrontation with a NATO ally. This creates a precedent — other NATO members watching closely.
verificationNote: Spain airspace closure confirmed by Spanish Defense Ministry statement, Euronews. Trump trade war threat confirmed by Times of Israel and NBC News.

text: Oil Market Intelligence: Day 31 oil surge to $114.90 Brent driven by three simultaneous catalysts: (1) Tabriz Petrochemical Company strike (energy infrastructure hit); (2) Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery fire (signal Iran will target Israeli energy too); (3) Trump Kharg Island seizure threat (market pricing in risk of even greater supply disruption). Goldman raised Brent forecast to avg $110 in March/April. Saudi oil officials cited by Bloomberg warn prices could exceed $180/bbl if disruptions persist through late April.
verificationNote: Brent $114.90 per CNBC March 30. Tabriz and Haifa strikes per Anadolu Agency and Jerusalem Post. Trump Kharg threat per Bloomberg and FT. Goldman forecast per CNBC.

text: Special Operations Monitor: IDF Alpinist Unit's cross-border operation from Syrian Hermon to south Lebanon is operationally significant. First time IDF publicly documented such a multi-domain cross-border maneuver. The operation reveals two things: (1) IDF is now treating the Syrian Hermon foothills as a forward staging area for Lebanon operations; (2) Hezbollah has maintained weapons caches in the Mount Dov buffer zone even under the Lebanon ground invasion. Bunkers and artillery found suggest Hezbollah prepared long-term stay-behind capability.
verificationNote: IDF Alpinist operation confirmed by IDF spokesperson, Times of Israel liveblog, Jerusalem Post, and JNS.

text: Aurora Intelligence: Day 31 escalation scorecard — (1) IDF strikes now targeting civilian economic infrastructure (Tabriz Petrochemical, Mehrabad Airport, power grid) alongside military targets; (2) Iran now striking partner-nation infrastructure (Kuwait power plant — 1 killed); (3) Trump threatening seizure of Kharg Island goes beyond any stated US war objective; (4) Spain-US rift is first direct NATO fracture. If all four continue simultaneously, Day 32-35 will see the war either substantially escalate or the Islamabad diplomacy process become urgent necessity.
verificationNote: All component facts confirmed by primary sources: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Euronews, Times of Israel Day 31 coverage.

text: CTP-ISW Day 31 (2 PM ET): IDF strikes in last 24h confirmed targeting Imam Hussein University (IRGC's main academic military institution), IRGC R&D ballistic missile complex for satellite launchers and missile systems, UAV engine production facility, and Sa'Iran Electronics Industries. Hassan Hassanzadeh (IRGC Rasul-Allah Corps Tehran commander) reported eliminated. Israel now shifting to economic targets: gas infrastructure and major steel facilities are next phase.
verificationNote: CTP-ISW assessment March 30. Targets confirmed by IDF spokesman and Times of Israel Day 31 liveblog. Target shift to economic infrastructure reported by Alma Research Center.

text: Alma Research Day 31 (6 PM): 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in two separate incidents (1 Indonesian at Adchit Al-Qusayr, 2 in vehicle near Bani Hayyan). Iran's joint military command declares private residences of US/Israeli officials 'legitimate targets.' Spain formally closed airspace to US military — first NATO member to defect from support. Iran publishes list of 23 universities in Israel, Lebanon, Iraq, Qatar, UAE, Kuwait as potential targets. UAE's Gargash demands compensation from Iran for pre-war 'deception.'
verificationNote: Alma Research March 30 18:00 daily report. All facts cross-referenced with Times of Israel and Al Jazeera Day 31 liveblogs.

text: HRW Report (March 30): Iran has used cluster munitions in ballistic missile attacks against Israeli civilian areas at least 3 confirmed times since Feb 28. Four Israelis confirmed killed: 2 men in Yehud (March 9), 1 man + 1 woman in Ramat Gan (March 18). Missiles carried 21-80 submunitions each. HRW reviewed 50+ videos. Iran's use of cluster munitions on civilian areas 'may amount to war crimes.' Israel does not intercept submunitions to conserve interceptors — 100s of submunitions have fallen over populated areas.
verificationNote: Human Rights Watch report published March 30. Primary source. Full report at hrw.org.

text: PM oil market summary Day 31: Brent settled $112.78/bbl (+41.3% from pre-war $79.84). WTI settled $102.88/bbl — first close above $100 since July 2022 (+36.2% from $75.51). Intraday high $116 on Trump's Truth Social 'obliterate' post. AAA gas national average $3.99/gal — up $0.87/gal from pre-war $3.12 (+27.9%). Goldman Sachs $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium embedded. Saudi officials privately warn $180+ if disruptions extend through May. April 6 Hormuz deadline remains key catalyst.
verificationNote: Oil settlement prices per CNBC and Fortune. AAA gas average per AAA March 30. Goldman risk premium from published Goldman Sachs research. Saudi officials per Bloomberg.

text: Iran War Live: Day 31 PM summary (2:16 PM PDT) — (1) HRW: Iran cluster munitions killed 4 Israeli civilians, may be war crimes; (2) 3 UNIFIL peacekeepers killed in Lebanon (deadliest UN day); (3) IDF Sgt. Liran Ben Zion (19) killed by Hezbollah anti-tank missile; (4) Rubio: Hormuz 'will reopen one way or another,' describes fractures in Iran; (5) Trump truth social: obliterate Iran's power plants/Kharg Island by April 6; (6) US Special Ops (Rangers, SEALs) arrive in Middle East; (7) Brent settles $112.78 — WTI first close above $100 since 2022; (8) AAA gas $3.99/gal.
verificationNote: Compiled from HRW report, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, CBS News, CNBC, and Al Jazeera Day 31 liveblogs.

text: Special Forces MENA: Pentagon confirms hundreds of Army Rangers and Navy SEALs have arrived in Middle East — no mission yet assigned. USS Tripoli (USS-7, carrying 31st MEU with F-35Bs, Ospreys, 3,500 sailors/Marines) confirmed operational in region. Total US troops in theater now exceeds 57,000 — largest US military buildup in Middle East since 2003. Potential missions include Strait of Hormuz escort operations and Kharg Island contingency plans. Ground ops decision remains with POTUS.
verificationNote: USS Tripoli arrival confirmed by CBS News and USNI News. Special Ops deployment confirmed by Middle East Monitor. US troop count from CENTCOM via Multiple news sources.
